spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (document.querySelector, translate(, drawElement) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Effectively no live controls — does not clear the interactivity bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (canvas, card, content, drawelement, html, render, select, serialisation, thumbnail).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (3 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: 5 raw hex colour(s) still in the style block. The design system requires CSS variables (`--bg-ivory`, `--text-charcoal` etc.)

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "HTML-in-canvas enables customizing the rendering of html using canvas with three new primitives: an attribute to opt-in canvas elements (layoutsubtree), methods to draw child elements (2d: drawElement". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Add live controls and a runtime hook
openQuestions.detail: Page must offer at least a try-it button that invokes the API and shows its return value or visible side effect; current state is closer to a static card.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Swap raw hex colours for CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Find the remaining hex literals in the inline `<style>` and replace each with the matching variable from `public/styles.css`. Re-check WCAG AA on any new pair you create.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("HTML-in-canvas enables customizing the rendering of html using canvas with three new primitives: an attribute to opt-in canvas elements (layoutsubtree), methods to draw child elements (2d: drawElementImage, webgl: texElementImage2D, webgpu:") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11588 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~3100 chars of script across 1 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 1 pass / 4 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
